name: North Savo (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the azure upper field, evoking the lakes of the Savo region
the golden lower field of the regional arms
red heraldic detail in the central charge

description: A swallow-tailed flag divided horizontally blue over yellow, charged centrally with the elaborate provincial arms of North Savo.
history: North Savo (Pohjois-Savo) became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic arms of the Savo province.

features.symbolism: a heraldic composition derived from the historic provincial arms of Savo, depicting a crowned figure with a bow and additional Savonian frontier motifs in gold, silver and red.
